koh — mosh over iroh

a peer-to-peer remote shell

koh — mosh, in Rust
over iroh

iroh p2p QUIC
no port · no SSH
v0.9.0

← → navigate · click any image to enlarge · everything here is real

what koh is & why

A remote shell that survives the real world

Over a laggy or mobile link, plain SSH hurts: every keystroke waits a full round-trip, and a sleep, IP change, or dropped link kills your session. koh keeps the shell instant and alive — building on a decade of prior art.

the baseline

OpenSSH

Secure & universal — but laggy echo on high-RTT links, dies on roam, and surviving a drop means a fixed port + tmux.

✓ everywhere   ✗ laggy   ✗ no roam
2012 · the ancestor

mosh

Added predictive local echo + roaming over UDP — instant feel, survives IP changes. But it bootstraps over an SSH login, has no detach/reattach, and is C++.

✓ echo   ✓ roam   ✗ needs SSH   ✗ no reattach
the p2p rewrite

koh

mosh's feel, from scratch in Rust over iroh p2p QUIC. Reachable by node-id — no port, no SSH — plus detach/reattach and an always-encrypted identity.

✓ echo   ✓ roam   ✓ no port   ✓ reattach   ✓ Rust
instant local echo
survives suspend / resume + IP change
no listening port, no SSH
detach → reattach
how it connects

Reachable by node-id — no port, no SSH

install · on the phone

Termux → cargo install koh

1 · Termux
the Android terminal
2 · pkg install rust
219 MB toolchain
3 · cargo install koh
compiles on-device

A native arm64 build — koh's full dep tree (iroh · ring · rustls · tokio) compiling on the phone.

step 1 · phone

Get your id

🔒 key encrypted at rest
Argon2id · AES-256-GCM
step 2 · host

Allow & serve

📷 scan the QR
allowlist is the only gate
step 3 · dial in

Run koh connect

Termux · Android
$ koh connect 03f0e0a2…aa9f4a01
koh id: ef580619…8f9b31f7
connecting to 03f0e0a2…aa9f4a01 …
connected. (Ctrl-^ then . to quit)
default · relays + DNS discovery
--direct ip:port · LAN
--relay-url · self-hosted
paste the server id — or scan its QR

the headline feature

Kill the network. It just resumes.

Wi-Fi off → resuming…
back → resumed, scrollback intact
QUIC path migration
the server saw no disconnect
vs ssh & mosh

The same shell, a different bet

kohmoshOpenSSH
reachp2p QUIC, node-idUDP over SSHTCP :22
no listening port✅❌❌
predictive echo✅✅❌
roam / survive IP change✅✅❌
detach → reattach✅❌❌
file transfer · tunnels❌❌✅
languageRust, forbid(unsafe)C++C
structure

One crate, a strict spine

client
input + Transport + predictor + render
server
PTY + emulator + Transport · detachable sessions
▲ built on ▲
predict
local-echo engine · standalone
terminal
vt100 screen + emulator
input
keystrokes + resize, synced
transport_iroh
endpoint · encrypted identity · datagrams
▲ over ▲
ssp
SyncState + Transport — a pure state machine (no sockets, clock, async)
wire
envelope · postcard codec · fragmenter
transport-agnostic core
only this edge touches iroh
deterministically testable under loss
what's next · exploratory

Where koh is headed

Native Bevy app

An Android app on bevy, reusing koh's terminal + input + predict + transport — no Termux.

Browser client · WASM

koh to wasm with iroh-in-the-browser — connect from a tab, nothing to install.

Voice terminal · Rig + TTS

A rig LLM agent + text-to-speech: speak commands, hear output read back — hands-free.

all reuse the transport-agnostic core
directions, not promises
